title: Colsterdale Halifax Memorial
crew_summary: 8 killed including pilot Sgt. Richard Ernest Charles Bacon

external_narratives.summary: Detailed account of Halifax JB926 from 1658 HCU crashing at Slipstone Crags on the night of 23/24 November 1943. The aircraft broke up in mid-air due to severe icing on a night cross-country exercise despite gale warnings. All eight crew killed.
key_details: Aircraft broke up in mid-air before impact
Port outer wing and both outer engines detached
Ice build-up on airframe was cause
CO allowed exercise despite gale warnings

external_narratives.summary: Crash site investigation record for Halifax JB926 at Slipstone Crags. Documents the crash location, wreckage distribution, and the structural failure sequence caused by severe icing conditions.
key_details: Part of broader Pennines crash sites database

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museums register entry for the Halifax Bomber JB926 memorial stone at Colsterdale, North Yorkshire.
RAF Commands forum discussion with historical details of the Halifax JB926 crash from RAF Riccall. Includes crew details and circumstances of the 1658 HCU training accident.

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: Photon OSM — Colsterdale valley at 54.2260 -1.7680; moor at 54.2268 -1.8312
location_confidence.verification_notes: INACCURATE. Previous coords (53.904, -1.354) ~36km SSE near Knaresborough suburbs. All sources converge on Colsterdale at ~54.226 -1.79 to -1.81. Hamlet node at 54.2272 -1.7942 used. Crash on moorland may be near moor centroid at 54.2268 -1.8312.
